data.title: Global Ship Lease Non-GAAP EPS of $2.32 beats by $0.01, revenue of $190.95M beats by $2.56M
data.description:
• Global Ship Lease press release (GSL): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.32 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $190.95M (+4.7% Y/Y) beats by $2.56M.
  • 4Q 2025 Adjusted EBITDA (a non-U.S. GAAP financial measure, described below)3 of $124.7 million. 
  • Added $1.26 billion of contracted revenues during 2025 and the first two months of 2026, bringing total contracted revenues as of December 31, 2025, as adjusted to include all charters agreed through February 28, 2026, to $2.24 billion, over a weighted average remaining duration of 2.7 years.

data.title: Revolut applies for U.S. bank charter as part of global expansion
data.description:
UK-fintech Revolut filed applications for a U.S. national bank charter as part of its planned expansion into North America in its aim to be a global banking platform, the company said on Thursday.

It filed applications with the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency and the Federal Deposit Insurance Corp. for a national bank charter to be called Revolut Bank US N.A.

Additionally, the firm named Cetin Duransoy as U.S. CEO, succeeding Sid Jajodia, who remains at the company as global chief banking officer.

"This charter will give us the direct control needed to innovate faster and deliver the Revolut experience to millions more Americans as we move toward our goal of 100M customers," said Revolut Co-Founder and CEO Nik Storonsky. Currently, the company serves more than 70M customers worldwide.

The bank charter will allow Revolut to operate across all 50 states under federal regulatory oversight, will give it direct access to payment rails, will enable it to directly offer insured deposits to U.S. customers and to offer personal loans and credit cards.

As of March 2026, Revolut operates in 40 markets globally, offering services including money transfers and currency exchange across borders between Revolut users, among other services. The company is aiming to expand into 30 new markets by 2030 and reach 100M customers by mid-2027.

The company's new U.S. CEO, Duransoy, most recently served as the U.S. CEO of Raisin, a fintech marketplace. He brings more than two decades of tech, payments, and finance industry experience to Revolut, the company said.

data.title: Grocery Outlet slides to an all-time low as promotions whack margins
data.description:
Grocery Outlet Holding Corp. (GO) slid in premarket trading Thursday after the company missed estimates with its fourth-quarter earnings report and set full-year profit guidance well below expectations ($0.45 to $0.55 vs. $0.82 consensus). A heavy reliance on promotions appears to be driving the company's margins lower. Looking ahead, Grocery Outlet (GO) outlined an optimization plan that will result in the closure of 36 financially underperforming stores and will take $110M of non-cash impairment charges.

Telsey Advisory Group downgraded Grocery Outlet (GO) to Market Perform from Outperform and cut its price target to $9 from $15. Analyst Joe Feldman said the rating downgrade reflects disappointing earnings results and 2026 guidance related to the loss of market share due to a combination of a difficult macro environment, the loss of its value proposition as the company shifted away from opportunistic purchases to prioritize higher in-stock levels on everyday items, and increased competition from the likes of Walmart (WMT) and Aldi that is likely to remain intense. "While the company is working with speed to rectify some of its self-inflicted issues, particularly its value proposition by shifting its focus back toward opportunistic products, it will take time and effort to recapture customers," warned Feldman.

Jefferies also downgraded Grocery Outlet (GO), moving to a Hold rating from Buy.  "Our prior optimism around a cleaner reset and faster execution recovery has faded. Instead, results point to ongoing execution missteps, a second restructuring in ~12 months, and heavier reliance on promotions to prop up volumes at the expense of margins and profit growth," highlighted analyst Corey Tarlowe. Limited upside is seen for the stock due to the recovery timing and earnings durability increasingly uncertain. Jefferies set a price target of $7 on GO.

Shares of Grocery Outlet (GO) were down 24.2% in premarket action on Thursday to trade at an all-time low of $6.67.

data.title: CorMedix reports mixed Q4 results; reaffirms FY26 outlook
data.description:
• CorMedix press release (CRMD): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.16 misses by $0.66.
  • Revenue of $128.62M (+312.1% Y/Y) beats by $1.6M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $77.2 million.
  • Cash and short-term investments, excluding restricted cash, at December 31, 2025 amounted to $148.5 million.
  • CorMedix reiterates previously established guidance for 2026, including net revenue of between $300 and $320 million vs. $308.96M consensus and adjusted EBITDA of between $100 and $125 million.
  • CorMedix announced in early February that its Board of Directors approved a share repurchase program, which authorizes the Company to repurchase up to $75 million of the Company’s outstanding common stock.

data.title: Iran war: South Korean lawmaker flags risks to chip industry
data.description:
The U.S.-Israel-Iran conflict could disrupt the supply of chipmaking materials and push electricity costs higher, a South Korean lawmaker warned on Thursday, according to multiple media reports.

"The semiconductor industry expressed concerns that rising oil prices could lead to higher electricity costs domestically, which could ultimately undermine the price competitiveness of semiconductors," lawmaker Kim Young-bae said after meeting with executives from companies such as Samsung Electronics (SSNLF) and SK Hynix (HXSC.F).

Kim said South Korean companies source key chipmaking materials from the Middle East, like helium, and any supply disruption will impact chip production.

"Logistics and transportation expenses will likely be the biggest issues rising from the situation in the Middle East," he said.

South Korea is "highly dependent" on the Middle East for 14 items used in chipmaking, including helium, bromine and chip inspection equipment, its industry ministry said. Some of these can be sourced domestically or from other countries.

But 54% of its naphtha imports travel through the Strait of Hormuz, and supplies could be hit if the Iran conflict is prolonged.

South Korea sources 70% of its oil from the Middle East. The government said it has enough oil reserves for now, but it plans to secure alternative supplies. Lawmaker Kim said he is working on a proposal to deploy the reserves as needed for each industry.

Some chipmakers are downplaying supply concerns. SK Hynix (HSXC.F) told Reuters it has "long secured diverse supply chains and sufficient inventory" of helium, because of which "there is almost no chance that ​the company will be affected."

Samsung (SSNLF), South Korea's largest chipmaker, declined to comment.

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing said it does not expect any significant impact currently, ‌and continues to closely monitor the situation.

Contract chipmaker GlobalFoundries (GFS) said it is "in direct contact with suppliers, customers and partners in the region" and "mitigation plans" are in place.

data.title: Oatly makes production investment amid rising plant-based drink demand
data.description:
Oatly Group AB (OTLY) confirmed a multi-year investment in its Landskrona production facility in Sweden to meet accelerating global demand for its plant-based drinks. European demand for plant-based drinks rose 6% in the last 12 months, and Oatly (OTLY) saw double-digit growth in the region over the same period.

The company said the expanded infrastructure will boost the site’s production capacity by more than 33% while also further reducing the climate impact of operations across three key areas.

The facility will see its production capacity increased to 200M liters per year from 150M liters, all within the same physical footprint. 

"We’re seeing growing demand for our products, so the time is right to upgrade our Landskrona site, which has performed fantastically well in recent years, both in stability of output and outstanding cost management. The Landskrona factory is a key site for us, not only because of our roots in Sweden, but also because it’s a fully owned, end-to-end production hub and home to many of our core functions. You can think of it as one big oat campus," highlighted Oatly (OTLY) SVP Sustainable Operations Simon Broadbent.

Shares of Oatly (OTLY) were up 1.8% in premarket trading to $11.40 vs. the 52-week range of $8.25 to $18.84.

data.title: Lilly launches program to drive employee access to obesity care
data.description:
Eli Lilly (LLY) announced on Thursday the launch of the Employer Connect platform, which enables employees to access its obesity therapy Zepbound at a lower cost through a network of pharmacies and a group of program administrators.

Employers participating in the platform will connect with more than fifteen independent program administrators, including Teladoc (TDOC), GoodRx (GDRX), and a network of dispensing pharmacies such as HealthDyne and CenterWell. 

Through this platform, the Indiana-based drugmaker plans to sell its popular GLP-1 medicine, Zepbound, in a single-patient delivery device called KwikPen to network pharmacies at a discounted price of $449 for all doses.

The choice of pharmacy and program administrator will determine the final cost to employers, and out-of-pocket expenses to employees will differ depending on dispensing and service fees charged by program administrators and employers’ cost-share model.

"By enabling coverage outside traditional benefit designs, we lower barriers to treatment and give employers greater control over how they support employee access to obesity care," said Kevin Hern, Senior Vice President, Lilly Employer.

data.title: Alpine Income Property Trust originates $32M loan for Georgia retail development
data.description:
• Alpine Income Property Trust (PINE) announced a $32M first mortgage loan investment, with $8.6M funded at closing.
  • The 24-month loan carries a 13% interest rate (including 1.5% paid-in-kind interest), stepping down to 11.50% current pay upon the borrower’s satisfaction of certain conditions.
  • The loan will finance development of an 11-acre, 101,000-sq-ft retail center with national investment-grade tenants and three outparcels. The retail center will also include a 128,500-sq-ft Target store currently in development.
  • The development is located within the 180-acre Covington Town Center master plan in Newton County, Georgia, ~35 miles from Atlanta and Hartsfield-Jackson International Airport.

data.title: Aptiv prices $1.6B upsized senior notes offering
data.description:
• Aptiv (APTV) priced an upsized private offering of $1.6B in senior notes, including $800M in 6.125% senior notes due in 2031 and $800M in 6.375% senior notes due in 2034.

  • The offering is upsized by $100M from the initial $1.5B size.

  • The offering is expected to close on March 18, 2026.

  • The net proceeds, plus term loan borrowings, will fund a dividend to Aptiv, leaving Versigent with $400M cash for general purposes post-spin-off and fees. The proceeds of the offering will be deposited into escrow for the holders of the notes.

data.title: USA Rare Earth to buy Texas Mineral Resources in $73M all-stock deal
data.description:
USA Rare Earth (USAR) said Thursday it agreed to acquire Texas Mineral Resources (TMRC) for ~3.8M shares of USAR common stock with an implied total deal value of ~$73M.

The acquisition makes USA Rare Earth (USAR), making it the sole operator of the Round Top heavy rare earth and critical minerals project in Texas, which the company said is "North America's richest known deposit of heavy rare earths and critical minerals."

Acquiring Texas Mineral Resources' (TMRC) 18.6% interest in the project will simplify governance, unify strategic decision-making, and align capital planning and execution under a single operator, the company said.

Under an accelerated mining plan, USA Rare Earth (USAR) said it expects that by 2030, it will extract ~40K metric tons/day of rare earth and critical mineral feedstock from Round Top by 2030.
